channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: UK public opinion turns more negative towards US role in world affairs amid Iran conflict, according to new poll
item.description: Due to restricted access to the full article text, a detailed rewrite cannot be produced from the original source. However, based on the headline and related reporting on similar Sky News polls concer...

item.title: Tiger Woods' Absence at Masters Draws Reflection and Concern
item.description: The absence of Tiger Woods at this year's Masters Tournament has cast a somber mood over Augusta National. While golfers compete for the coveted green jacket, much of the conversation centers around W...

item.title: Nigerian Navy to Form Multinational Maritime Task Force for Gulf of Guinea Security
item.description: The Nigerian Navy don announce say dem wan set up one Combined Maritime Task Force (CMTF) to help safeguard the Gulf of Guinea, one important waterway for world trade. Vice Admiral Idi Abbas, wey be t...

item.title: Kenyan man sentenced in the US for unlawful firearm possession
item.description: A Kenyan man living in the United States has been sentenced to prison by American authorities after being found guilty of illegally possessing a firearm. According to court documents, the man was arre...

item.title: A Review of 'Missing Sam': Exploring a Marriage Shaken by Abduction and the Aftermath
item.description: In Thrity Umrigar's novel *Missing Sam*, the focus is on the complexities of a marriage rocked by a sudden and mysterious abduction. The narrative begins with a fight between Samantha O'Malley and Ali...

item.title: Australia capable of deploying naval assets to Middle East but Indo-Pacific remains priority, says Defence Chief
item.description: Australian Defence Force chief Admiral David Johnston has stated that Australia is fully capable of sending a naval warship to the Middle East if requested by the government. While confident in the AD...

item.title: PSC Officials Respond to Contempt Allegations Over DPP Removal Process
item.description: Senior officials at the Public Service Commission (PSC), including chairperson Francis Meja, are in a legal tussle over allegations of contempt of court in connection with proposed changes to the Offi...

item.title: World Bank Funds Sh71bn Upgrade of Isiolo-Mandera Road to Boost Trade and Connectivity
item.description: The World Bank has sanctioned Sh71 billion to improve the Isiolo-Mandera road corridor in northern Kenya. This major funding will cover the rehabilitation of 508 kilometres of road and the installatio...

item.title: Iowa Congressional Candidate Reflects on 9/11 and Religious Diversity
item.description: Sarah Trone Garriott, an Iowa state legislator and ordained Lutheran minister running for a battleground congressional seat, shared her reflections on the September 11, 2001, attacks. In an opinion pi...

item.title: Phivolcs Records Multiple Ash Emissions at Kanlaon Volcano Under Alert Level 2
item.description: State volcanologists from the Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (Phivolcs) recorded a series of ash emissions at Kanlaon Volcano on Negros Island yesterday. The first emission happene...

item.title: Pawan Khera seeks transit anticipatory bail in Assam forgery case, calls it politically motivated
item.description: Congress leader Pawan Khera has called the forgery and criminal conspiracy case filed against him by the Assam Police a politically motivated act. The case arises from allegations made by Khera about ...

item.title: Tanzania losing Sh1 trillion yearly from illicit alcohol as industry and government call for joint action
item.description: Tanzania is facing a big challenge with illicit alcohol that is costing the government more than Sh1 trillion in lost taxes every year. According to Tanzania Breweries Limited Managing Director Michel...

item.title: Health department warns of lead poisoning risks from informal three-legged cooking pots
item.description: Health authorities in South Africa have raised concerns about the potential dangers of using informal three-legged cooking pots, especially the silver-coloured ones commonly used for potjiekos. The De...

item.title: Congress readies for April 14 hearing on Sara Duterte impeachment amid Supreme Court petitions
item.description: The House Committee on Justice is finalizing preparations for its April 14 impeachment hearing on the complaints against Vice President Sara Duterte while also working on its formal response to petiti...

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs Appeals Conviction in Court
item.description: The legal battle surrounding Sean 'Diddy' Combs takes another turn as his defense team files an appeal for his conviction. This case, which has drawn significant public interest, focuses on a legal ma...

item.title: PH Embassy in Beirut advises Filipinos on repatriation due to escalating conflict in Israel
item.description: The Philippine Embassy in Beirut is advising Filipino nationals in Lebanon to consider repatriation, following the ongoing military escalations in Israel. The embassy highlighted the increasing instab...

item.title: Jason Day Makes Strong Push for Masters Lead as McIlroy Shares Early Lead
item.description: Jason Day, former world No.1, made a strong push for contention at the 2026 US Masters, shooting a solid 69 in the opening round. Starting off shaky with a bogey on the third hole, the Queenslander se...

item.title: Western Cape provides R22 million in fodder relief for drought-stricken livestock farmers
item.description: The Western Cape Department of Agriculture has approved R22 million in relief funding to assist livestock farmers who are struggling due to prolonged dry conditions. This financial aid will be used to...

item.title: From Personal Memories to Political Cinema: Bollywood’s Shift Towards Nationalism
item.description: The article explores the profound influence of Bollywood on the author’s early life in Fiji and its later political implications. Growing up in the Methodist quarters of Dilkusha, Fiji, the author dis...

item.title: Russia implements two-day ceasefire in Ukraine for Orthodox Easter
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has declared a temporary ceasefire in Ukraine to coincide with Orthodox Easter, from 4pm on 11th April to the end of 12th April, with the expectation that Ukraine will...
